title: Unitary heating element and heater assemblies, cartridges, and e-vapor devices including a unitary heating element
abstract: A heating element for an e-vapor device includes a planar portion including at least one filament. The filament may define an air channel through the planar portion. The heating element may include first and second lead portions extending away from the planar portion. The planar portion, the first lead portion, and the second lead portion may be a unitary body.
claims: Claims IT IS CLAIMED: 1. A heating element for an e-vapor device, comprising: a planar portion including at least one filament, the filament defining an air channel through the planar portion; and first and second lead portions extending away from the planar portion, the planar portion, the first lead portion, and the second lead portion being a unitary body. 2. The heating element of claim 1, wherein the filament defines the air channel through a central area of the planar portion. 3. The heating element of claim 1, wherein the filament includes stainless steel. 4. The heating element of claim 1, wherein the first and second lead portions extend away from the planar portion in a direction that is substantially perpendicular to the planar portion. 5. The heating element of claim 5, wherein a tip of the first lead portion and a tip of the second lead portion are bent in a direction that is parallel to the planar portion. 6. The heating element of claim 1, wherein the filament follows a circuitous path and defines the air channel. 7. The heating element of claim 6, wherein a width of the filament varies along the circuitous path. 8. The heating element of claim 7, wherein the width of the filament gradually increases in a direction away from the air channel. 9. The heating element of claim 6, wherein the filament includes a plurality of filament portions that are substantially u-shaped, and the plurality filament portions are connected to one another at end sections of each u-shape. 10. The heating element of claim 9, wherein a space between adjacent ones of the plurality of filament portions gradually increases in a direction away from the air channel. 11. A cartridge for an e-vapor device, comprising: a housing including a reservoir portion and a connector portion, the connector portion being configured to connect the cartridge to a power supply section; and a heater assembly including a heating element disposed in the housing, the heating element including, a planar portion in fluid communication with the reservoir portion, and first and second lead portions extending away from the planar portion. 12. The cartridge of claim 11, wherein the planar portion, the first lead portion, and the second lead portion are a unitary body. 13. The cartridge of claim 11, wherein the planar portion includes at least one filament, the filament defining an air channel through the planar portion. 14. The cartridge of claim 11, wherein the reservoir portion includes a porous substrate, and an inner tube defining an airway through a central area of the reservoir portion and defining a cavity between the housing and the inner tube, the cavity being configured to store a pre-vapor formulation, the porous substrate being in fluid communication with the cavity. 15. The cartridge of claim 14, wherein the porous substrate includes an opening aligned with the air channel, and the porous substrate contacts the planar portion of the heating element. 16. The cartridge of claim 14, wherein the reservoir portion includes a gasket configured to provide the fluid communication between the porous substrate and the storage portion. 17. The cartridge of claim 16, wherein the gasket includes an opening that fits around the inner tube, and the gasket is configured to provide the fluid communication via at least one aperture in the gasket. 18. The cartridge of claim 17, wherein the at least one aperture is four apertures in a diamond configuration around the opening. 19. The cartridge of claim 11, wherein the heater assembly includes a support disposed in the housing and supporting the heating element. 20. The cartridge of claim 19, wherein the support has an end surface including a through hole extending through a central area of the support, a first slot, and a second slot, the first slot and the second slot being disposed on the end surface at opposing sides of the through hole, the first lead portion extending through first slot, the second lead portion extending through the second slot. 21. The cartridge of claim 20, wherein the first lead portion and the second lead portion are bent in a direction that is substantially parallel to the planar portion. 22. The cartridge of claim 21, wherein the support includes a hollow conductive rivet extending through the through hole such that the conductive rivet is electrically connected to the second lead portion and electrically isolated from the first lead portion. 23. The cartridge of claim 19, wherein the support has a male thread to engage with a female thread of the housing. 24. The cartridge of claim 23, wherein the support includes at least two recesses disposed on opposite sides of a lateral surface of the support, the reservoir portion includes at least two connection points, and the connector portion includes at least two flexible tabs extending into the at least two recesses and releasably engaged with the at least two connection points. 25. The cartridge of claim 11, wherein the connector portion includes a conductive inner wall, and the first lead portion is electrically connected to the inner wall of the connector portion. 26. A heater assembly for an e-vapor device, comprising: a heating element including, a planar portion including at least one filament, the filament defining an air channel through the planar portion, and first and second lead portions extending away from the planar portion; and a support supporting the heating element, the support including a first slot and a second slot, the first lead portion extending through the first slot, the second lead portion extending through the second slot. 27. The heater assembly of claim 26, wherein the support is substantially cylindrical and has an end surface including a through hole a first slot, and a second slot, the through hole extending through a central area of the support, the first slot and the second slot being disposed on the end surface at opposing sides of the through hole, the first lead portion extending through first slot, the second lead portion extending through the second slot. 28. The heater assembly of claim 27, wherein the support has a male thread around circumference lateral surface of the support and at least two rectangular shaped recesses disposed in the male thread on opposing sides of the lateral surface of the support, the at least two recesses accommodating at least two tabs of a connection portion of the e-vapor device. 29. The heater assembly of claim 27, wherein the first lead portion and the second lead portion are bent in a direction that is substantially parallel to the planar portion. 30. The heater assembly of claim 29, wherein the support includes a hollow conductive rivet extending through the through hole such that the conductive rivet is electrically connected to the second lead portion and electrically isolated from the first lead portion. 31. The heater assembly of claim 27, wherein the through hole is aligned with the air channel. 32. An e-vapor device, comprising: a cartridge including, a housing including a reservoir portion, and a heater assembly disposed in the housing, the heater assembly including a heating element, the heating element including, a planar portion in fluid communication with the reservoir portion, and first and second lead portions extending away from the planar portion; and a power supply section configured to selectively supply power to the heating element. 33. The e-vapor device of claim 32, wherein the cartridge and the power supply section are releasably connected. 34. The e-vapor device of claim 32, wherein the cartridge and the power supply section are in a unitary housing.
